title: betula pumila var. typica
intro: Betula pumila var. typica, also known as dwarf birch, is a shrub variety native to the Arctic tundra in North America and Eurasia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of salicin and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Betula pumila var. typica include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
